vulnerability_detection.justification: Both vulnerabilities identified precisely: XSS via the |raw filter (critical) and missing CSRF token (high). The audit correctly names the attack vector — attacker-controlled URL/form submission injecting HTML via the unescaped preview — and shows clear understanding of why |raw is dangerous while Twig's default auto-escaping is not.

fix_correctness.justification: XSS fix removes |raw and relies on Twig's built-in auto-escaping — addresses the root cause, not just the symptom. CSRF fix uses Symfony's csrf_token() function and correctly advises server-side validation with isCsrfTokenValid(), which is the complete, correct fix. Both fixes would pass a security review.

fix_completeness.justification: Every identified vulnerability has a corresponding fix in the output file: |raw removed on line 19, CSRF hidden field added on line 6. AUDIT.md documents both changes with before/after context.

regression_avoidance.justification: All existing functionality preserved: the preview div still renders the message (now escaped), form pre-population from query params is intact for all three fields, and the form structure, labels, action, and method are unchanged.

false_positive_avoidance.justification: No false positives. The three auto-escaped expressions — name in input value, email in input value, message in textarea — are all explicitly called out as safe in AUDIT.md. The agent correctly understands that Twig auto-escapes by default and only |raw is the actual danger.

framework_idiom.justification: Fixes are idiomatic Symfony/Twig throughout: uses Twig's native auto-escaping (simply dropping |raw rather than adding a manual sanitiser), uses Symfony's built-in csrf_token() Twig function, and references the correct controller-side validation API. An expert in this stack would write it exactly this way.

expectations_met: FIXES: Removes the |raw filter from the preview — output line 19 uses auto-escaped {{ app.request.get('message') }}
FIXES: Adds CSRF token to the form — <input type="hidden" name="_token" value="{{ csrf_token('contact') }}"> present on line 6
PRESERVES: Message preview feature still works (now shows escaped text)
PRESERVES: Form pre-population from query params still works for name, email, and message
PRESERVES: Form structure and labels remain intact
DOES NOT: Flag {{ app.request.get('name') }} as XSS — AUDIT.md explicitly marks it safe
DOES NOT: Flag {{ app.request.get('email') }} as XSS — AUDIT.md explicitly marks it safe
DOES NOT: Flag {{ app.request.get('message') }} in the textarea as XSS — AUDIT.md explicitly marks it safe
AUDIT: Correctly identifies |raw as the XSS vector, not the auto-escaped expressions
AUDIT: Explains that Twig auto-escapes by default, making |raw (and autoescape false) the only dangerous patterns
additional_observations: The audit also advises the controller-side CSRF validation call (isCsrfTokenValid), which is necessary for the CSRF fix to be complete end-to-end — a good proactive detail.
